incorrects.description: Student selected 16: added 6 + 10 instead of multiplying

params.description: The multiplication symbol highlights between 6 and 10.

steps.text: That times sign means multiply, not add. Skip-count by 10s: 10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60. Which number did you land on after 6 jumps?
You skipped one too many times. Count carefully: 6 groups of 10. That means 6 jumps, not 7.

incorrects.description: Student selected 50: stopped one count short

steps.text: You stopped too soon. Count all 6 groups: 10, 20, 30, 40, 50, and one more. What comes after 50?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: System demonstrates skip-counting by 10s six times: 10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60. Each count pulses briefly as the number appears.

steps.text: Let me show you. 6 groups of 10. I skip-count by 10s, six times: 10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60. After 6 jumps of 10, I land on 60. That's the product. 6 times 10 equals 60.
